MOTION / Emergency Motion To Compel Discovery Responses; Request For Deemed Admissions; Request For Sanctions; Order Re: Guardian Ltd
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
THOMAS GODDARD self-represented plaintiff
Ariel Valerio-Meek,. Esq., of CONSTANGY, BROOKS, SMITH & PROPHETE, LLP., Tel:6196056171 for defendant.

Hearing held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from Plaintiff regarding the Court's tentaive ruling. 1. The entire ruling taking the motion off calendar based on "impermissible stacked motion". 2. The finding that courtesy copies were not provided when they were sent via U.S. Mail on September 20, 2025, but were returned by postal service due to ADA accommodation denial 3. The finding that discovery responses render the motion moot when responses consist of boilerplate objections lacking substantive answers. 4. The failure to address Requests for Admission that should be deemed admitted due to inadequate responses
5. The application of a "stacked motions" rule that does not exist in the California Rules of Court or Superior Court of San Francisco Local Rules. The Court denied the various request by the Plaintiff. The matter discussed. The Court having reviewed the pleadings, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Thomas Goddard's motion to compel discovery responses; request for deemed admissions; request for sanctions; and order compelling completion of Guardian Life LTD forms is OFF CALEDNAR.

First, Plaintiff failed to provide courtesy copies of all papers filed in support of this motion. (See SFLA, rule 2.7.) On September 19, 2025, the court continued this motion to allow Plaintiff to provide complete courtesy copies, informing him the motion may be taken off calendar if Plaintiff did not provide the courtesy copies. Plaintiff failed to provide the courtesy copies. Contrary to his protestations, Plaintiff is not excepted from the courtesy copy rule. The motion is off calendar.

Second, the motion is off calendar for the separate reason that it is an impermissible stacked motion. Plaintiff's motion includes a request for an injunction granting him relief on the merits, a request for a particular trial date, and various discovery requests. Such disparate requests will not be herd in one motion. At least one of the requests (trial date) is not properly addressed to this department. And discovery motions should not be stacked. Stacked motions, such as this one, may be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party. Indeed, here, Plaintiff's requests for relief would have been better presented had they not been stacked because Plaintiff could have focused each request on the appropriate statute and relevant standards, including the provisions regarding sanctions.

Third, the record shows Defendant did provide responses to these discovery requests. Thus, this motion is moot. The record supports an order declining to find the RFAs admitted. Plaintiff is ordered to review and comply with the Civil Discovery Act before filing additional motions regarding these discovery requests.

Order signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION / Omnibus Motion For Procedural Consolidation, Expedited Proceedings, And Order Compelling Completion Of Disability Benefits Forms; Request For Summary Judgment Schedule
Off Calendar Sep-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - PLAINTIFF THOMAS GODDARD's Motion For Procedural Consolidation, Expedited Proceedings, And Order Compelling Completion Of Disability Benefits Forms; Request For Summary Judgment Schedule.

Plaintiff Thomas Goddard's omnibus motion is DENIED.

The motion improperly combines several unrelated and duplicative requests for relief in a single motion. The request to file an amended complaint is improper since plaintiff already scheduled a motion seeking that relief, which is set for September 30, 2025. The request to compel defendants to execute an insurance claims form appears to be an unsupported request for a mandatory preliminary injunction. (See People v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 266, 283 ["In general, when considering a request for a preliminary injunction, the trial court weighs two interrelated factors. The first is the likelihood the party seeking relief will prevail on the merits, and the second is the relative interim harm to the parties if the preliminary injunction is granted or denied."].) Plaintiff has not made the necessary showing. Plaintiff's discovery requests are the subject of another discovery motion that will be heard on October 8, 2025, if plaintiff complies with SF Local Rule 2.7B and provides courtesy copies. At this point of the litigation, there is no basis for the court to issue any advisory discovery rulings. Lastly, it is premature for the court to issue a ruling regarding an unfiled motion for summary judgment.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
